title: Day 8 Daily Test — Will Have (Future Perfect)

questions.hindi: कल तक, मैं report खत्म कर चुका होऊँगा।
options: By tomorrow, I will finish the report.
By tomorrow, I will have finished the report.
By tomorrow, I have finished the report.
By tomorrow, I had finished the report.
questions.answer: By tomorrow, I will have finished the report.

questions.hindi: अगले साल तक, वह MBA पूरी कर चुकी होगी।

By next year, she will have completed her MBA.

questions.answer: By next year, she will have completed her MBA.

questions.hindi: June तक, university ने final results declare कर दिए होंगे।
options: By June, the university will declare the results.
By June, the university will have declared the final results.
By June, the university has declared the results.
By June, the university declared the results.
questions.answer: By June, the university will have declared the final results.

questions.hindi: अगले दस साल में, वह अपना business शुरू कर चुकी होगी।
options: In the next ten years, she will start her own business.
In the next ten years, she will have started her own business.
In the next ten years, she has started her own business.
In ten years, she starts her own business.
questions.answer: In the next ten years, she will have started her own business.

questions.hindi: March तक, मैंने car loan चुका दिया होगा।
options: By March, I will pay off my car loan.
By March, I will have paid off my car loan.
By March, I have paid off my car loan.
By March, I paid off my car loan.
questions.answer: By March, I will have paid off my car loan.

questions.sentence: By 2030, we will have expanded to ten country.
questions.correct: By 2030, we will have expanded to ten countries.
options: By 2030, we will have expanded to ten country.
By 2030, we will have expanded to ten countries.
By 2030, we expanded to ten countries.
By 2030, we will expanded to ten countries.
questions.answer: By 2030, we will have expanded to ten countries.

questions.sentence: I will have be finished by tomorrow.
questions.correct: I will have finished by tomorrow.
options: I will have be finished by tomorrow.
I will have finished by tomorrow.
I will be finished by tomorrow.
I have be finished by tomorrow.
questions.answer: I will have finished by tomorrow.
questions.explanation: Active Future Perfect: will have + V3, no 'be'।

questions.sentence: The reports will have been approve by noon.
questions.correct: The reports will have been approved by noon.
options: The reports will have been approve by noon.
The reports will have been approved by noon.
The reports will be approved by noon.
The reports have been approve by noon.
questions.answer: The reports will have been approved by noon.

questions.sentence: By next year, they will have became market leaders.
questions.correct: By next year, they will have become market leaders.
options: By next year, they will have became market leaders.
By next year, they will have become market leaders.
By next year, they became market leaders.
By next year, they will become market leaders.
questions.answer: By next year, they will have become market leaders.

questions.sentence: She won't have been complete the training.
questions.correct: She won't have completed the training.
options: She won't have been complete the training.
She won't have completed the training.
questions.answer: She won't have completed the training.
questions.explanation: Active Future Perfect Negative: won't have + V3।

questions.question: Which sentence shows the correct use of 'in' with Future Perfect?
options: I will have finished in tomorrow.
In 5 years, I will have become a manager.
I will finish in 5 years time.
questions.answer: In 5 years, I will have become a manager.
questions.explanation: 'In + time period' works with Future Perfect to express a future goal.

questions.question: 'Won't have' is the contraction of:
questions.explanation: 'won't have' = 'will not have' — Future Perfect Negative।

questions.question: Which verb form follows 'will have'?
questions.explanation: will have + V3 (past participle) = Future Perfect।

questions.question: 'By the time' is used to indicate:
questions.explanation: 'By the time + clause' means before or at the point the clause happens।

questions.question: The future perfect passive structure is:
questions.explanation: Passive Future Perfect: will have been + V3।

questions.question: 'He will have written the book by December.' Which part is the time reference?
questions.explanation: 'By December' is the future time reference for Future Perfect।

questions.question: Which is an example of Future Perfect in a career context?
options: By 2028, I will have led a team of 50.
questions.answer: By 2028, I will have led a team of 50.

questions.question: 'Eventually' in Future Perfect sentences means:
options: at some point in the future
questions.answer: at some point in the future
questions.explanation: 'Eventually' = at some point in the future, used with Future Perfect.

questions.question: Which is the WRONG form?
'Before the show starts, the director _____ rehearsed all scenes.'
questions.explanation: 'Before + future event' triggers Future Perfect: will have + V3।
